methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Project Omega, a seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Stafford Sheehan, has raised $12M to date. The valuation landscape includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers spanning the nuclear sector, including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Applied Atomics.

Stage-progression methodology applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier to the $12M raised proxy, implying a $30M valuation at the next funding stage. Peer-median analysis was not applicable: while 4 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 datapoints met narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables. A sector-wide median would introduce unwarranted heterogeneity across nuclear subsegments and valuation tiers.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M to $34M, centered on $30M, derived from the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include (1) reliance on a single triangulation method, reflecting the sparse comparable universe at seed stage in nuclear energy; (2) sensitivity to the 2.5x multiplier assumption, which may not reflect Project Omega's specific risk profile, technology readiness, or capital efficiency relative to peer trajectories; and (3) absence of bottom-up revenue or technical-milestone anchors. Broader market data or internal milestones would narrow this range materially.
